title: storage — field classification
description: Every column across the four owned entities classified per foundation's five-tier taxonomy. File bytes themselves are treated as `restricted` by default — their content is user-supplied and can contain any tier of PII.

confidential: File.filename (may reveal user intent or subject via naming)
File.custom_properties (arbitrary bag — may include EXIF geolocation + author info)
File.sha256 (content hash; a colliding upload confirms possession of the content)
restricted: File byte content (the actual bytes on disk — user-supplied, can contain anything)
File.path (tenant-scoped storage key; revealing it enables path-guessing attacks even though signed URLs remain required)
ChunkedUpload.upload_url (a live upload URL is a temporary write credential)
SignedUrlAudit.signature_hash (revealing the hash enables replay-attack analysis of the signature scheme)
regulated_minor: Any File where owner_id resolves to a minor (via user::User.age_gate=minor_under_consent_age). Files inherit their owner's regulatory context. Also captures Files fileable_type=Stackra\User\Models\Minor when that submodel exists.
regulated_health: Files uploaded to a health-classified fileable (e.g. medical_record kinds when we implement HIPAA support). Not currently applicable; reserved.
regulated_financial: Files of kind `receipt`, `invoice`, `contract`, `tax_document` when tenant is in a tax jurisdiction (JP, DE, FR) requiring 7-year retention. Byte-level content classified as restricted.
audit_log: Every SignedUrlAudit row — by design an audit record.
FileUploaded + FileErased events written to audit::AuditLog.
always_redacted: File.path is HIDDEN on the wire (x-wire.hidden). Never surfaces on any tenant-facing endpoint. Platform-admin endpoints require ?include=storage_internals to opt in.
File.sha256 is HIDDEN on the wire.
ChunkedUpload.upload_url appears ONLY on the initiate-response body, then HIDDEN on subsequent reads.
SignedUrlAudit.signature_hash is HIDDEN even from tenant admins.

notes: File bytes themselves are NOT stored in the database — they live on the storage backend. Byte content classification is a policy declaration about what can be uploaded (governed by the #[FileKind] allowed_mimes list + tenant's data-classification policy for user-supplied content) — not a column classification.

PDF metadata + EXIF exposure: `File.custom_properties` may contain EXIF GPS coordinates from photo uploads. The FileObserver::created hook is configured to strip GPS EXIF by default; tenants can opt in via `storage.preserve_exif_gps` setting.
Ownership drift: when `File.owner_id` becomes null (owner user deleted), the file inherits tenant-level ownership. Compliance queries treat the tenant as the residual data controller.
